canonical_definition: As defined in the provided literature, token copying is the task of identifying and reproducing a specific token from a given position, such as the first or last, within an input prompt. This behavior is also described more broadly as the "‘copying’ or ‘recalling’ of a single or multiple tokens from the prompt." The source material frames it as a "seemingly simple" task that can be challenging for Transformer-based LLMs, resulting in errors. It is studied alongside the behavior of counting. According to one paper, model collapse is a phenomenon that can cause performance degradation on this task.
papers: Compact Proofs of Model Performance via Mechanistic Interpretability
Linking In-context Learning in Transformers to Human Episodic Memory
Transformers need glasses! Information over-squashing in language tasks

definitions.text: The task of identifying and reproducing a specific token from a given position (e.g., first or last) in the input prompt.
